0.group_name_GPT: Family Travel and Children’s Experience Vlog
0.group_des_GPT: This cluster centers on family-oriented travel and child-focused experience videos. The most representative rows show children moving through indoor amusement or educational facilities, theme parks, playgrounds, shrines, zoos, arcades, and Japanese tourist sites. Representative files include 2uncle987-20250326095233-871834575765944 and 2uncle987-20251023040938-788537414265266.
0.group_name_Gemini: Parent-Child Travel and Family Lifestyle Vlogging
0.group_des_Gemini: This group centers around family lifestyle content, specifically outdoor activities, amusement park trips, and international or local family travel involving young children. Visually, it features multi-generational dynamics (parents, children) exploring public spaces, interactive play areas, and historical sights. A defining characteristic is the high-energy, cheerful presentation of family milestones, establishing an intimate, relatable connection through shared parenting experiences. Highly representative examples include 2uncle987-20250326095233-871834575765944 (exploring Kids Plaza Osaka) and 2uncle987-20251023040938-788537414265266 (children trying professional uniforms in Japan). It differs from other groups by its outdoor, family-centric orientation and absence of overt tech or pure studio debate.

1.group_name_GPT: Skincare Promotion with Demonstration and Comedic Challenge
1.group_des_GPT: This cluster is defined by beauty and skincare product promotion built around facial close-ups, before/after comparisons, product packaging, cream or serum application, and playful group interaction. Representative files include 2uncle987-20251020164148-1458252856165442 and 2uncle987-20250601095023-25611298551845742.
1.group_name_Gemini: Lifestyle Commercialization and Beauty/Skincare Brand Endorsement
1.group_des_Gemini: This group represents the commercial and monetization dimension of lifestyle influencers, focusing primarily on beauty, skincare, and personal care brand collaborations. Structurally, these videos follow a standardized product demonstration format: starting with a high-contrast split-screen "Before/After" skin texture comparison, introducing product packaging, and showing the influencer applying the product to their skin. To maintain authenticity, creators incorporate casual indoor backgrounds and humorous elements like "NG" bloopers or lighthearted banter with friends. Key representative examples are 2uncle987-20251020164148-1458252856165442 (skincare challenge with NG takes) and 2uncle987-20250727175654-793021490480798 (demonstrating Dr. Douxi serum). Unlike Group 0's focus on family life, this group leverages personal transformation as persuasive social proof for commercial endorsement.

2.group_name_GPT: Costco Food Find and Two-Person Tasting Review
2.group_des_GPT: This cluster captures Costco food discovery videos that move from warehouse product selection to home or studio tasting. The closest cases show a woman picking up refrigerated or frozen food at Costco, presenting packaging, then sitting at a table with another woman to cook, split, taste, and react. Representative files include cocowine0205-20250629000106-2083469552451893 and cocowine0205-20250521182504-945150058316974.
2.group_name_Gemini: Savory Retail Food Exploration and Social Mukbang
2.group_des_Gemini: This group captures the consumer culture surrounding mega-retailers (primarily Costco) in Taiwan, focusing on the unboxing, preparation, and shared tasting of savory food products. The visual narrative typically begins from a first-person or over-the-shoulder perspective inside a warehouse aisle where the creator picks up a packaged savory item, followed by a transition to a bright domestic dining room. Here, two female creators co-present the cooked food, breaking it open to showcase textures, and tasting it together with highly expressive facial reactions. Highly representative examples are cocowine0205-20250629000106-2083469552451893 (reviewing Johnsonville sausages) and cocowine0205-20250521182504-945150058316974 (unboxing seafood buns). It is distinguished from Group 4 (sweet treats) by its savory focus, and from Group 6 (pure info) by its emphasis on dual-host social interaction and visceral eating experiences.

3.group_name_GPT: Viral Food Experiment and Exaggerated Eating Reaction
3.group_des_GPT: This cluster is dominated by creator-led food experiments, mukbang-style tasting, and viral trend imitation. Central videos often begin with split-screen references to other influencers or food trends, then show unusual combinations, texture close-ups, large bites, and exaggerated reactions. Representative files include 602avenue-20250918114407-4401971093414194 and 602avenue-20250802082520-2309855456177572.
3.group_name_Gemini: Viral Food Challenges and Exaggerated Reaction Culture
3.group_des_Gemini: This group represents the hyper-expressive, attention-driven "Reaction Economy" on social media, focusing on viral food trends, unconventional flavor combinations, and culinary challenges. A primary stylistic indicator is the structural use of a split-screen or Picture-in-Picture (PiP) layout, where the creator reacts in real-time to external videos of global food trends before replicating the experience. The food items are often unusual, visually shocking, or highly trending, and tasting is accompanied by exaggerated facial expressions of shock, disgust, or intense pleasure. The most representative cases are 602avenue-20250918114407-4401971093414194 (reacting to viral Korean instant rice cakes) and 602avenue-20250802082520-2309855456177572 (challenging a mix of coffee and instant noodles). This group differs from standard food reviews (Group 2 & 4) because it prioritizes performative shock value, novelty, and digital intertextuality over standard product satisfaction.

4.group_name_GPT: Costco Bakery and Dessert Table Review
4.group_des_GPT: This cluster is a more specific Costco review format centered on bakery, pastry, cake, cookie, pancake, and dessert products. The strongest examples show creators locating a boxed bakery item inside Costco, then opening, cutting, tearing, or tasting it indoors at a table. Representative files include cocowine0205-20250414093504-2142528699827578 and cocowine0205-20250216054206-702177962983851.
4.group_name_Gemini: Confectionery Consumerism and Sweet Dessert Reviews
4.group_des_Gemini: This group focuses on the specialized sub-genre of dessert and pastry review videos, reflecting the distinct lifestyle aesthetic associated with sweet indulgence. Stylistically identical to Group 2 in its reliance on Costco warehouse sourcing and a dual-host domestic setup, it is isolated by its exclusive focus on sweet bakery items, pastries, cakes, and gift sets. The visual climax always involves a tactile close-up, such as slicing a cake or tearing open a pastry to display inner fillings (e.g., mousse, cream). Representative examples include cocowine0205-20250414093504-2142528699827578 (tasting Blueberry Blossom Pastries) and cocowine0205-20250723095431-707134112391831 (breaking open Cheese Butterfly Crisps). From a sociological standpoint, it separates itself from savory reviews (Group 2) as a distinct content vertical tailored toward leisure aesthetics and sweet-tooth consumer demographics.

5.group_name_GPT: Tech Host Explainer and Gadget Demonstration
5.group_des_GPT: This cluster centers on technology commentary delivered by a middle-aged male host, often wearing glasses and a flat cap, speaking directly to the camera while product graphics, smartphone screens, headphones, earbuds, watches, or app interfaces appear. Representative files include 3ctim-20250705185106-1826553664695824 and 3ctim-20250219172139-1661366341964183.
5.group_name_Gemini: Professional Tech Journalism and Consumer Electronics Dissemination
5.group_des_Gemini: This group embodies the highly specialized, information-dense domain of tech reviews and consumer electronics journalism. The aesthetic is strictly structured: a middle-aged male host speaking directly into a centered camera in a studio or retail store, flanked by tech boxes, utilizing precise hand gestures and frequent high-fidelity graphical overlays (e.g., brand logos, software app interfaces, specification charts). The tone is authoritative yet energetic, focusing on features, software configurations, and user experience of gadgets like smartphones, wireless earbuds, and smartwatches. Leading examples are 3ctim-20250705185106-1826553664695824 (explaining Nothing Phone features) and 3ctim-20250219172139-1661366341964183 (demonstrating CMF Buds Pro 2). It stands in sharp contrast to all other groups due to its absolute lack of domestic/lifestyle elements, its masculine-coded tech studio setting, and its focus on analytical product utility over emotional or aesthetic consumption.

6.group_des_GPT: This cluster represents fast, catalogue-like Costco weekly special reports. The closest rows open with a graphic title card, Costco logo, cartoon character, or weekly discount framing, then proceed through rapid cuts of many products across aisles, freezers, pallets, refrigerators, and shelves. Representative files include cocowine0205-20250929110746-1695715211451972 and cocowine0205-20250910235315-1455287069559402.
6.group_name_Gemini: Automated Consumer Informationalism and Rapid Price Cataloging
6.group_des_Gemini: This group represents an un-individualized, highly functional form of content production centered on pure information dissemination and price cataloging for budget-conscious consumers. Unlike standard vlogs, these videos completely omit the influencer's face and physical presence, instead utilizing a first-person point-of-view (POV) shopping cart perspective moving through retail spaces (specifically Costco). The structure consists of a rapid-fire montage of jump cuts (changing every 1.5-2 seconds), with each shot meticulously centered on a product packaging and its corresponding price tag on the shelf. The prominent representative videos are cocowine0205-20250929110746-1695715211451972 (Costco Weekly Special Report) and cocowine0205-20250910235315-1455287069559402 (Weekly Special showing price tags). It differentiates itself from all other groups by its complete erasure of the creator's persona, serving strictly as a digital utility catalog or price aggregator.

7.group_des_GPT: This cluster is defined by a studio podcast or talk-show format featuring professional microphones, headphones, seated hosts, rapid reaction cuts, laughter, and a recurring large cartoonish mask or mascot head. Representative files include yga0721-20250609090720-1402024218067507 and yga0721-20250523150647-1454968552999354.
7.group_name_Gemini: Studio Talk Shows, Collective Banter, and Masked Comedy Performance
7.group_des_Gemini: This group captures the highly institutionalized studio entertainment show format, characterized by collaborative storytelling, casual banter, and masked character comedy. The visual setting is fixed: a well-equipped indoor studio where multiple hosts sit on sofas or chairs, wearing professional over-ear headphones, and speaking directly into high-end studio microphones. A unique sociological feature of this group is the presence of co-hosts or guests wearing large, cartoonish, exaggerated masks (such as the Big Head Buddha or Dama style), which serves as a visual gimmick to enhance comedic tension and protect identities while building a specific channel subculture. The editing relies on fast pacing, alternating close-ups of laughing faces, and text-heavy overlays. Key examples are yga0721-20250609090720-1402024218067507 (studio chat with a cartoon mask) and yga0721-20250815001530-1555000902224470 (hosts laughing with a blue-haired masked figure). It differs from Group 8 by its collective studio architecture, and from Group 5 by its emphasis on interpersonal humor over tech data.

8.group_des_GPT: This cluster centers on a single young woman in tight close-up, usually indoors, looking directly into the lens and performing a personal or comedic monologue through facial expressions, eye contact, hair-touching, cheek-touching, pouting, smirking, and subtle hand gestures. Representative files include 0_shufen-20251218102358-1212158867689248 and 0_shufen-20250905012518-1294397242515994.
8.group_name_Gemini: Intimate Talking-Head Selfie Vlogs and Parasocial Daily Musings
8.group_des_Gemini: This group represents the most raw, minimal-production format on social media: the intimate, selfie-angle talking-head vlog. Visually, it is defined by tight, close-up shots of a single female creator looking directly and continuously into the camera lens, holding the device herself in a casual domestic environment (e.g., lying on a bed or sitting in a bedroom) with minimal editing. The narrative is unscripted and highly personal, consisting of daily storytelling, personal venting, or mock-complaining about life anecdotes, where the creator uses subtle, exaggerated facial expressions (exasperation, pouting) for comedic or emotional connection. Leading examples include 0_shufen-20251218102358-1212158867689248 (selfie-style close-up discussing a personal story) and 0_shufen-20251219105044-890347023736561 (close-up venting with exasperated expressions). This group is structurally unique from all others due to its extreme reduction of cinematic production, its lack of external products, and its reliance on pure parasocial intimacy and raw facial performance.
